mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 408039.0M as of Oct 2017 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL growth < 2% annualized) indicates monetary policy tightening without price pressures, which constrains equity valuations and produces negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% and CPIAUCSL at 229.177 as of Oct 2017
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than either single-series IC (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, signaling expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength that supports corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a contraction in residential investment demand that precedes broader economic weakness and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 654.0K units as of Sep 2017
failure_condition: When HOUST declines > 10% MoM, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and retail sales growth (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating synchronized labor and demand strength that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires joint confirmation of both labor and demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132989.0K and RSAFS at 408039.0M as of Oct 2017
failure_condition: The interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than either single-series IC (p > 0.10), or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in < 50% of instances when both PAYEMS and RSAFS growth accelerate.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation regime (CPIAUCSL growth < 1.5% annualized), rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp in 3 months) signal policy error and produce negative 5-day equity returns. The mechanism is regime-conditional and reverses in high-inflation environments.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL annualized growth < 1.5%
failure_condition: In low-inflation regimes, FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp produce negative 5-day returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, signaling robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations and produces positive 60-day forward returns. The mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13502.4B (2017 dollars) as of Q2 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances.
